missed_changes: Strong new open-source-AI advocacy (2024-07-23): praises 'uncensored' Mistral/Llama models, argues closed-source AI limits innovation and isn't demonstrably safer — a distinct AI-policy stance beyond the crypto×AI-provenance thesis

Newly vocal optimism on payments and stablecoins for mainstream adoption ('Payments... the pieces are finally coming together'; stablecoins 'critical for mainstream adoption') — not listed in the prediction's excited sectors
Deep engagement with PoS slashing / consensus security and the Compound 'governance attack' analysis plus DUNA/DAO-governance framework — a governance-mechanics focus the prediction didn't anticipate

Endorsement of specific DeFi/AMM designs (DELV/Hyperdrive fixed-variable AMM, Morpho) and security-tooling (Halmos symbolic testing, Vyper/snekmate) — concrete protocol-level enthusiasm beyond the stated sectors
notes: Nearly every predicted stance is directly reaffirmed in the Apr–Sep 2024 window, often emphatically: the Jolt Alpha launch and repeated ZK posts; the 'mystery meat' provenance worry and crypto-as-AI-safety-layer post; sybil-filtering and private-identity comments; blob-target/scaling advocacy; EVE Frontier and ProofOfPlay on-chain-games content; River/Propeller and the Gmail-ossification critique for open networks; an extended multi-tweet anti-memecoin thread; and a full CSX token-design talk hammering 'you design a protocol, not a token' with Maker/DAI and ETH as canonical exemplars. The risk posture (skeptical-in-analysis, optimistic-in-action, no market timing) is consistent throughout. Only the Bitcoin-security-budget concern is never revisited as a concern — Bitcoin appears only as a neutral example of issuance paying for security — so it is scored absent. No contradictions found.
